note: jquery_ui_slider is an asset-library provider (a compatibility shim) with no config UI, permissions, services, or PHP API, so all cases are `recipe` (response-graded), not `execution` (live-state).

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal 11 module I have a render array in `$build`. Attach the jQuery UI Slider library provided by the jquery_ui_slider module so the slider widget works on the page. Show the exact PHP line.
What is the exact library identifier (the `provider/library-name` string) that the jquery_ui_slider module declares for the jQuery UI Slider asset library?
Why does a contrib module like jquery_ui_slider exist for Drupal 9.2+/10/11 when the jQuery UI Slider used to just be in core? Explain in one or two sentences.
evals.expected_output: jQuery UI was deprecated and progressively removed from Drupal core, so this module re-provides the jQuery UI Slider asset library for code that still needs it.

evals.prompt: Which module does jquery_ui_slider depend on to obtain the underlying vendored jQuery UI JavaScript files? Name it.
evals.expected_output: It depends on the base `jquery_ui` module (drupal/jquery_ui), which vendors the underlying jQuery UI files.

evals.prompt: Give the Composer command to add the jquery_ui_slider module to a Drupal site.
evals.expected_output: composer require drupal/jquery_ui_slider (which also pulls in drupal/jquery_ui).

evals.prompt: After enabling jquery_ui_slider, where in the Drupal admin UI do I configure it, and what permissions does it add? Answer accurately.
evals.expected_output: There is nothing to configure: the module has no configuration UI, no settings page, and no permissions. You just enable it and attach the `jquery_ui_slider/slider` library.

evals.prompt: In a custom theme I want the jQuery UI Slider library loaded whenever my own library loads, instead of attaching it in PHP. How do I reference jquery_ui_slider's library as a dependency in my theme's *.libraries.yml?
evals.expected_output: Under your library's `dependencies:` list in the theme's *.libraries.yml, add `- jquery_ui_slider/slider`.
